life_expectancy.dataset_description: WHO Life Expectancy dataset. Predicts life expectancy (regression). Contains country, status (Developed/Developing), and numeric health/economic indicators. Very few categorical columns — mainly 'Country' (high cardinality) and 'Status' (2 values).
must_do: encode_categorical with ordinal for tree-based efficiency
life_expectancy.notes: Dataset domain: public health/economics. Categorical nature: Country is nominal high-cardinality, Status is binary. High-cardinality columns: Country (~193 unique values). ML model encoding guidance: Ordinal encoding is efficient and appropriate. Trees make binary splits at thresholds, so ordinal values work well regardless of whether categories have a natural order. This also reduces dimensionality compared to one-hot, which can improve performance with high-cardinality features.
